title: Uber and Rivian Announce $1.25B Robotaxi Deal
text: Uber Technologies plans to invest up to $1.25 billion in electric vehicle maker Rivian Automotive as part of a deal to deploy up to 50,000 robotaxis in several countries through 2031. The partnership expects to deploy 10,000 fully autonomous R2 robotaxis in the first phase of R2 robotaxi deployment, with the option to negotiate the purchase of 40,000 more in 2030. An initial $300 million investment from Uber to Rivian is expected soon following the deal's signing, subject to regulatory approval. Four other investment tranches will occur subject to hitting certain milestones by unspecified dates through 2031. Initial deployments are expected to begin in San Francisco and Miami in 2028 and will expand to 25 cities by 2031. The R2 robotaxis are expected to be available exclusively through Uber's ride-hailing and delivery platform in 25 cities across the U.S., Canada and Europe. The deal was announced on March 19, 2026, and represents a significant partnership to advance autonomous vehicle technology for ride-hailing services.
